title: 10 best herbal teas for adrenal fatigue

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic and Stress-Reducing Properties: Ashwagandha tea helps to reduce stress levels by modulating the body's response to stress, allowing it to cope better with adrenal fatigue caused by chronic stress.

Anxiolytic and Sedative Effects: The herb's anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) and sedative properties help to calm the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety that often accompanies adrenal fatigue.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Ashwagandha tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to adrenal fatigue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera, also known as ashwagandha, teas helps with adrenal fatigue because it aids in reducing cortisol levels and stress response. This herb has adaptogenic properties, allowing it to help the body adapt to stress and promote balance in the adrenal glands. By reducing cortisol production, ashwagandha teas can help alleviate symptoms of adrenal fatigue, such as fatigue, insomnia, and decreased productivity. Additionally, it can help improve sleep quality and overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for adrenal fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: Withanolides are a group of alkaloids that help reduce cortisol levels and alleviate adrenal fatigue by regulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis and promoting a state of relaxation and reduced stress.

Withaferin A: Withaferin A is a withanolide that has been shown to increase the expression of antioxidant enzymes and reduce oxidative stress, which is a common contributor to adrenal fatigue, by neutralizing free radicals and promoting a healthy balance of the body's antioxidant system.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of triterpenoid saponin, help to reduce inflammation and promote adrenal function by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting a healthy balance of the body's inflammatory response.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their adaptogenic properties, which help to balance the body's response to stress.
Leaves: They are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and promote relaxation.
Seeds: They are used for their adaptogenic and anti-anxiety properties, which can help to reduce stress and promote a sense of calm.
remedy_recipe: Select 2 teaspoons of dried withania somnifera root powder or 3 tablespoons of fresh root for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Combine the withania somnifera powder or fresh root with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Serve the tea warm or at room temperature after allowing it to cool for 2 to 3 minutes.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ginseng tea helps the body adapt to stress by regulating cortisol levels, reducing inflammation, and increasing resistance to physical and emotional stressors, which is particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing adrenal fatigue.

Antioxidant activity: Ginseng tea's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can damage adrenals and disrupt their function, promoting overall health and well-being in individuals with adrenal fatigue.

Immune-modulating properties: Ginseng tea has been shown to boost the immune system by increasing the production of white blood cells, which is essential for individuals with adrenal fatigue who often experience compromised immunity.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng, also known as ginseng, teas helps with adrenal fatigue because it supports the body's natural stress response and promotes adrenal function. The herb contains compounds that help regulate cortisol levels and improve energy production, which is often impaired in individuals with adrenal fatigue. By stimulating the body's energy and vitality, Panax ginseng teas can help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, brain fog, and decreased productivity associated with adrenal exhaustion. This can lead to improved overall well-being and a sense of balance.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins have anti-inflammatory and adaptogenic properties, which help in reducing oxidative stress and promoting the body's natural response to stress, alleviating adrenal fatigue symptoms.

Eleutherosides: These triterpenoid saponins have adaptogenic and antioxidant properties, which help in regulating the body's response to stress, supporting adrenal function, and improving overall energy levels.

Panaxynol: This terpenoid compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with adrenal fatigue, promoting overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Panax ginseng are used to make teas for adrenal fatigue due to their high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to help alleviate fatigue and improve overall well-being.
Leaves: The leaves of Panax ginseng are used to make teas for adrenal fatigue due to their ability to provide a gentle, subtle energy boost without the stimulating effects of other ingredients.

Barks: The barks of Panax ginseng are used to make teas for adrenal fatigue due to their potential to help regulate cortisol levels and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial for individuals suffering from adrenal fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried panax ginseng roots weighing 5 grams for a standard serving size.
Rinse the ginseng roots under cold running water for 30 seconds to remove impurities.
Combine the rinsed ginseng roots with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes allowing the ginseng to infuse its flavor and medicinal properties.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the ginseng solids.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Golden Root tea helps regulate stress response by adapting to changing circumstances, reducing the body's burden on adrenal glands and promoting balance in the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Golden Root tea's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to Adrenal Fatigue by protecting adrenal gland function.

Hormone-regulating effects: Golden Root tea has been shown to regulate cortisol levels, support thyroid function, and modulate other hormonal imbalances that are often associated with Adrenal Fatigue, helping to restore balance and reduce symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rhodiola rosea, also known as golden root, teas helps with adrenal fatigue because it supports the body's natural ability to cope with stress. The adaptogenic properties of Rhodiola rosea allow it to regulate cortisol levels, promoting a healthy balance between energy and fatigue. By reducing stress and anxiety, Rhodiola rosea teas enable the adrenal glands to recover and function more efficiently, leading to improved energy, mental clarity, and overall well-being in individuals with adrenal fatigue. This makes it a popular choice for those seeking natural relief.
remedy_constituents: Rosavin: It helps reduce oxidative stress and improves energy metabolism in the body, which is often impaired in adrenal fatigue.
Salidroside: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting recovery of adrenal function.
Tyrosol: It acts as a neurotransmitter precursor, supporting the production of dopamine, norepinephrine, and epinephrine, which are often depleted in adrenal fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of rhodiola's active compounds, which help to reduce stress and improve mental performance.
Leaves: They contain rosmarinic acid, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation.
Stems: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to protect against oxidative stress and support adrenal function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest rhodiola rosea roots and rhizomes in early fall when foliage starts to turn yellow brown.
Clean and dry the harvested rhodiola roots and rhizomes to prevent mold growth.
Chop the dried rhodiola roots and rhizomes into small pieces to increase surface area.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of chopped rhodiola roots and rhizomes in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to serve the rhodiola rosea tea immediately.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Siberian Ginseng tea helps to adapt the body's response to stress, promoting balance and reducing the negative impact of cortisol on the adrenals.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the adrenal glands, which can contribute to fatigue, brain fog, and other symptoms associated with adrenal fatigue.
Antioxidant activity: Siberian Ginseng tea is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the adrenals from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy adrenal function.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eleutherococcus senticosus, also known as Siberian ginseng, teas helps with adrenal fatigue because of its adaptogenic properties. It stimulates the production of cortisol and other hormones, helping the body cope with stress and promoting balance in the adrenal glands. Regular consumption of this herbal tea can aid in reducing fatigue, improving mental clarity, and enhancing overall resilience to physical and mental demands. By supporting the body's natural response to stress, Eleutherococcus senticosus tea may help alleviate the symptoms of adrenal fatigue and promote a more balanced state of well-being.
remedy_constituents: Eleutheroside E: It helps with adrenal fatigue by enhancing the body's adaptogenic response, reducing stress, and improving overall resilience to physical and mental stressors.
Eleutheroside B: It helps with adrenal fatigue by supporting the adrenal glands and stimulating their function, which can help to increase the production of cortisol and other essential hormones.
Oleanolic acid: It helps with adrenal fatigue by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to adrenal exhaustion and other fatigue-related symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of eleutherosides, a compound responsible for the herb's adaptogenic properties, which help alleviate adrenal fatigue symptoms.
Barks: The bark contains eleutherosides, which aid in reducing stress and improving energy levels, making it a popular choice for adrenal fatigue teas.
Stems: The stems of Eleutherococcus senticosus also contain eleutherosides, contributing to their adaptogenic and stress-reducing effects when used in teas for adrenal fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried eleutherococcus senticosus root and place it in a clean glass container.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and slowly pour it over the dried root in the container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the desired flavor and potency are achieved.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean container.
Discard the solids and let the tea cool to a comfortable drinking temperature before serving.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps reduce cortisol levels and alleviate stress, allowing the adrenal glands to recover and function properly again.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, helping to protect the adrenal glands from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to adrenal fatigue.
Triterpenoids: Have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe and calm the adrenals, reducing symptoms such as anxiety, insomnia, and fatigue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with adrenal fatigue because of its adaptogenic properties and ability to balance cortisol levels. The root of the plant contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that can help regulate the body's response to stress, thereby alleviating symptoms of adrenal fatigue such as fatigue, insomnia, and mood swings. By supporting the adrenal glands and promoting a sense of calm, licorice teas can help individuals recover from adrenal exhaustion and regain their energy and vitality. It also supports the body's natural detoxification processes.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, helps with adrenal fatigue by inhibiting the enzyme 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, which breaks down cortisol, allowing the body to retain more cortisol and alleviate adrenal exhaustion.
Licoricidin: Licoricidin, a flavonoid glycoside, helps with adrenal fatigue by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the adrenal glands, promoting their function and reducing fatigue.

Licopyranocoumarin: Licopyranocoumarin, a phenolic compound, helps with adrenal fatigue by exhibiting antioxidant properties, protecting the adrenal glands from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps regulate the body's response to stress and supports adrenal function.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and saponins that may help reduce cortisol levels and promote relaxation.
Barks: May help reduce inflammation and improve cortisol regulation due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra from a trusted supplier or grow your own in well-draining soil.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces weighing 1-2 grams per 8 oz cup of water.
Steep the chopped root pieces in 8 oz of boiling water for 5-7 minutes in a heat-resistant cup.
Strain the tea into a separate cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before serving and consuming it in moderation due to potential side effects.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Valerian root tea helps to adapt the body's response to stress, reducing cortisol levels and promoting a sense of calm, which is particularly beneficial for individuals with adrenal fatigue who often experience chronic stress.

Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Valerian tea's sedative and anxiolytic properties can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, allowing the body to rest and recover from periods of adrenal exhaustion.

Hormone regulation: Valerian root contains compounds that have been shown to regulate hormone production, including cortisol and adrenaline, which can become imbalanced in individuals with adrenal fatigue, leading to symptoms such as fatigue, insomnia, and mood disturbances.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, teas helps with adrenal fatigue because of its adaptogenic properties, which allow it to support the body's natural response to stress. The tea's calming effects on the nervous system help to reduce cortisol levels, promoting a state of balance and relaxation. By reducing stress and promoting relaxation, valerian teas can help alleviate the symptoms of adrenal fatigue, such as fatigue, insomnia, and mood disturbances, allowing the adrenal glands to recover and function more efficiently.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic Acid: This valerenic acid acts as a GABA receptor agonist, which helps to calm the nervous system and reduce cortisol production, thereby alleviating adrenal fatigue symptoms.

Valeranone: Valeranone is a sesquiterpene that exhibits sedative and anxiolytic properties by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and reducing stress response, making it beneficial for adrenal fatigue recovery.

Valerian Root Flavonoids: These flavonoids, including isorhapontigenin and valerenic acid derivatives, possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with adrenal fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: These are the primary part used due to their high concentration of valerenic acid and valepotriates, which have been shown to have adaptogenic and calming effects.
Leaves: They are used in teas to provide a milder, less sedative effect compared to the roots, promoting relaxation without drowsiness.
Stems: The stems are sometimes used in combination with the roots or leaves to enhance their adaptogenic properties and promote balance in the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of valeriana officinalis roots and stems from a trusted source and clean them thoroughly.
Chop the valeriana roots and stems into small pieces to release their active compounds and prepare for steeping.
Combine the chopped valeriana with 1 liter of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to extract the herbs.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids and filter out impurities.
Discard the solids and serve the valeriana tea hot or let it cool to room temperature for consumption.

remedy_properties: Cooling effect: Peppermint tea's cooling effect helps to reduce cortisol levels, which can become elevated due to adrenal fatigue, and promotes relaxation and reduced stress.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often a contributing factor to adrenal fatigue, and promote overall well-being.
Digestive soothing: Peppermint tea's ability to soothe digestive issues, such as IBS and bloating, helps to alleviate symptoms of adrenal fatigue, including constipation, diarrhea, and stomach upset.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with adrenal fatigue because it has a unique ability to stimulate the digestive system and promote a sense of calm. The menthol content in peppermint tea helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles, which can be beneficial for individuals experiencing adrenal fatigue. Additionally, peppermint tea has been shown to improve mental clarity and focus, allowing the body to recover from prolonged periods of stress. This can be particularly helpful in managing the symptoms of adrenal fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol has a potential role in reducing stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to adrenal fatigue.
Linalool: Linalool, a terpene in peppermint, has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate the symptoms of adrenal fatigue, such as fatigue and mood disturbances.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the adrenal glands from oxidative stress and promote their recovery.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for teas to help with adrenal fatigue because they contain menthone, which helps to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
Rhyzomes: Used for teas to aid in adrenal fatigue recovery due to their high content of menthol, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce stress.
Leaves: Used for teas to address adrenal fatigue because they contain a variety of essential oils, including menthol and menthone, which can help to balance the body's energy and reduce fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of fresh mentha x piperita leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves for tea preparation.
Steep the herb in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to separate the tea from the solids.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of honey or other sweetener to taste if desired for a sweeter flavor.
Serve the herbal tea immediately and consume within 30 minutes for optimal flavor and health benefits.

remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (calming) effect: Chamomile tea helps reduce anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate adrenal fatigue by allowing the adrenal glands to rest and recover.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the body, which is a common symptom of adrenal fatigue and can contribute to chronic fatigue, insomnia, and other symptoms.

Hormone-balancing effects: Chamomile tea contains flavonoids that have been shown to regulate hormonal imbalances, including cortisol levels, which can become disrupted in individuals with adrenal fatigue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, teas helps with adrenal fatigue because of its calming and adaptogenic properties. The tea's flavonoids and terpenoids promote relaxation and reduce stress, allowing the body to recover from the exhaustion caused by adrenal fatigue. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate symptoms such as insomnia, anxiety, and digestive issues, which are common in individuals with adrenal fatigue. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Matricaria chamomilla teas can help alleviate symptoms and support adrenal function.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This phenolic compound helps with adrenal fatigue by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels.

Luteolin: This flavonoid flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to alleviate adrenal fatigue symptoms such as fatigue and brain fog by reducing inflammation and promoting a balance in the body's energy production.

β-Nerolol and β-Bisabolol (bisabolol): These sesquiterpenes have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects that help to calm the nervous system, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation, which is essential for managing adrenal fatigue.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part due to their high apigenin and luteolin content, which may help reduce stress and promote relaxation.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make teas due to their gentle and soothing effects, which may aid in reducing cortisol levels and promoting adrenal gland recovery.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make teas for their high oil content and potential anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate adrenal fatigue symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried flowers of matricaria chamomilla, ensure they are free from contamination and pesticides.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a fine mesh sieve to remove the flowers and excess particles.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature for consumption within 10-15 minutes.
Store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours in an airtight container.

remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: English lavender tea has been shown to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which is essential for managing the stress associated with adrenal fatigue.

Adaptogenic properties: Lavender contains adaptogens like linalyl acetate, which help the body adapt to stress by reducing cortisol levels and promoting a sense of calm, thereby alleviating adrenal fatigue symptoms.

Sedative properties: The sedative compounds in lavender tea, such as linalool, can help regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of sleep, which is often disrupted in individuals with adrenal fatigue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, teas helps with adrenal fatigue because of its calming and balancing effects on the body. The herbal tea's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a soothing impact on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate adrenal fatigue. By promoting relaxation and regulating cortisol levels, Lavandula angustifolia tea can help alleviate symptoms associated with adrenal fatigue, including fatigue, insomnia, and mood disturbances. This makes it a valuable natural remedy for individuals experiencing adrenal exhaustion.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Linalool, a terpene found in lavender tea, may help alleviate symptoms of adrenal fatigue by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, which can contribute to the development of adrenal fatigue.
Luteolin: Luteolin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the adrenal glands, a common factor in adrenal fatigue.

Linalyl acetate: Linalyl acetate, a sesquiterpene found in lavender tea, has been reported to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative effects, which can help alleviate stress and promote relaxation, ultimately supporting adrenal health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and calming properties, helping to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
Flowers: Known for their sedative and calming effects, which can help alleviate anxiety and promote better sleep.
Leaves: Also contain antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote overall adrenal function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days to reduce moisture.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their aromatic compounds and oils.
Strain the tea and let it cool before serving to preserve the delicate flavor and properties of lavandula angustifolia.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Lemon verbena tea helps to adapt the body's response to stress, reducing the negative impact of cortisol on the adrenal glands and promoting a sense of calm and balance.

Antioxidant activity: Lemon verbena tea's high antioxidant content helps to mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to adrenal fatigue and other symptoms such as fatigue, insomnia, and mood changes.

Anxiolytic properties: Lemon verbena tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which is essential for individuals with adrenal fatigue who often experience feelings of stress and overwhelm.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena officinalis, also known as lemon verbena, teas helps with adrenal fatigue because of its adaptogenic properties and ability to regulate the body's response to stress. The tea's high levels of antioxidants and flavonoids help to calm the nervous system, reducing cortisol levels and promoting a balanced hormonal response. Additionally, Verbena officinalis has been shown to have a stimulating effect on the adrenal glands, helping to increase the production of cortisol and other essential hormones, which can become depleted in cases of adrenal fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Verbascoside: A phenylethanoid glycoside that has shown adaptogenic properties, helping to reduce stress and promote the body's natural response to fatigue.
Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with adrenal fatigue.
Iridoid glycosides (e.g., verbascoside and aucubin): These compounds have been shown to have a potential adaptogenic effect, reducing stress and promoting recovery from fatigue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and adaptogenic properties, Verbena officinalis leaves help to balance and calm the adrenal glands.
Roots: The roots of Verbena officinalis contain adaptogenic compounds that help to reduce stress and fatigue by supporting adrenal function.
Flowers: The flowers of Verbena officinalis possess calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and balance the adrenal system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning when the dew is still present.
Gently rinse the verbena plant material in cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried verbena plant material or 2 tablespoons of fresh plant material with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the verbena mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse.
Strain the verbena tea and discard the solids before serving the tea hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with adrenal fatigue because it supports the body's natural detoxification processes, specifically targeting the liver and kidneys to remove excess hormones and toxins that can contribute to adrenal exhaustion. The tea's diuretic properties also help flush out waste products, reducing overall cortisol levels and promoting a sense of calm and balance in the body. Additionally, dandelion's adaptogenic properties enable it to harmonize with the body's natural rhythms, helping to regulate hormone production and mitigate the symptoms of adrenal fatigue.
remedy_properties: Diuretic Properties: Dandelion tea acts as a natural diuretic, helping to increase urine production and reduce water retention, which is common in adrenal fatigue patients who often experience bloating and swelling.

Adrenal Support: Dandelion root contains compounds that have been shown to support the adrenal glands, which are responsible for producing stress hormones like cortisol. This can help alleviate symptoms of adrenal fatigue such as exhaustion, brain fog, and insomnia.

Antioxidant Properties: Dandelion tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by chronic inflammation, a common underlying factor in adrenal fatigue. Antioxidants also support the body's natural detoxification processes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds, dandelion roots help to reduce stress and promote adrenal function.
Seeds: High in vitamin E and omega-3 fatty acids, dandelion seeds support adrenal health by reducing inflammation and improving cortisol regulation.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the herb in hot water for exactly 10 minutes at 200°f to release bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup to remove solids completely.
Let the tea cool to room temperature or drink it warm immediately after preparation time.
Consume 2-3 cups of this herbal tea per day for at least 6 weeks to notice improvements in adrenal function.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn teas helps with adrenal fatigue because it provides a gentle, yet potent, adaptogenic boost to the body. The herbal tea's flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) work synergistically to calm the nervous system, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation. This allows the adrenal glands to function more efficiently, reducing fatigue and promoting a sense of balance and well-being. Additionally, hawthorn's antioxidant properties help protect the adrenals from oxidative stress, supporting their natural ability to regulate energy production and hormonal balance.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic flavonoids: Hawthorn tea's adaptogenic flavonoids help to normalize cortisol levels, reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which is beneficial for individuals with adrenal fatigue.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds in hawthorn tea may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can exacerbate adrenal fatigue symptoms, such as fatigue, brain fog, and mood disturbances.

Cardiac glycosides: Hawthorn's cardiac glycosides have been shown to improve blood flow to the heart and increase the strength of heart contractions, which can be beneficial for individuals with adrenal fatigue who may experience cardiovascular problems due to chronic stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots are used to make tea as they contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and stress on the adrenal glands.

Barks: The barks of the hawthorn tree are used to make tea due to their ability to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and calm the nervous system, all of which can be beneficial for individuals with adrenal fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of crataegus monogyna in early summer for optimal potency and flavor.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried crataegus monogyna leaves with boiling water to create a strong infusion.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the bitter-sweet flavors and nutrients to meld together harmoniously.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids and retaining the precious liquid gold.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming, enjoying the soothing and invigorating effects of this adrenal fatigue remedy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yerba mate teas helps with adrenal fatigue because it contains a unique combination of caffeine, theobromine, and other nutrients that work together to stimulate the adrenal glands. The gentle, sustained energy boost provided by yerba mate can help alleviate feelings of exhaustion and lethargy, while its adaptogenic properties allow it to support the body's natural response to stress. Additionally, yerba mate's rich antioxidant content helps protect the adrenals from oxidative damage, promoting optimal function and recovery.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic compounds: Yerba mate tea contains adaptogenic compounds like caffeine, theobromine, and theophylline, which help the body adapt to stress by increasing resilience and reducing the negative effects of cortisol.

Antioxidant-rich: Yerba mate tea is rich in antioxidants, including vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, and other polyphenols, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by adrenal fatigue.

Catechin content: Yerba mate tea contains catechins, specifically epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), which can help regulate cortisol levels, improve insulin sensitivity, and support overall energy production.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yerba mate plant are used to make teas that help alleviate stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate adrenal fatigue.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of yerba mate contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to reduce cortisol levels and promote relaxation.
Barks: Yerba mate barks are used in teas to support the body's natural response to stress and help regulate adrenal function.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried ilex paraguariensis leaves for every 8 oz of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 7-10 minutes to release the active compounds and flavors.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large pitcher or individual cups.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature for at least 30 minutes to enhance bioavailability.
Store the cooled tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with adrenal fatigue because its adaptogenic properties allow it to balance the body's response to stress, calming an overactive nervous system. The tea's antioxidants and flavonoids also help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the adrenal glands, promoting a healthy cortisol cycle. Additionally, Maidenhair tree contains adaptogenic compounds that stimulate the production of adrenal hormones, supporting the body's natural response to stress and fatigue. This can lead to improved energy levels, reduced fatigue, and enhanced overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic compounds: Maidenhair tree tea contains adaptogens like catechins and flavonoids, which help the body adapt to stress by regulating cortisol levels and promoting a state of balance in the body.

Antioxidant activity: The high antioxidant content in Maidenhair tree tea helps protect the adrenal glands from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healthy gland function.

Stress-reducing flavonoids: Flavonoids like quercetin present in Maidenhair tree tea have anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety associated with adrenal fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help reduce inflammation and support adrenal function.
Bark: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, it helps to stabilize blood sugar levels and calm stress responses.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers per eight ounces of water.
Heat the water to a temperature of 212 degrees fahrenheit in a medium-sized pot.
Steep the ginkgo biloba mixture for fifteen minutes to allow for optimal extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool for at least five minutes before consuming with caution.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock teas helps with adrenal fatigue because of its ability to nourish and support the adrenal glands. The tea's rich antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the body, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate adrenal fatigue. Additionally, burdock root has been shown to improve circulation and promote healthy liver function, both of which are essential for proper adrenal gland function. By promoting balance and harmony in the body, burdock teas may help to alleviate symptoms of adrenal fatigue such as fatigue, brain fog, and mood disturbances.
remedy_properties: Inulin: Inulin helps to regulate blood sugar levels, which is important for individuals with adrenal fatigue who often experience blood sugar imbalances and mood swings. It also supports gut health, which is essential for hormone production and regulation.

Polyphenols: Burdock's polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, both of which can exacerbate adrenal fatigue symptoms such as fatigue, insomnia, and mood changes.

Inulin-type fructans: These prebiotic fibers help to promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which is critical for hormone production and regulation. A healthy gut microbiome is essential for supporting adrenal function and reducing symptoms of adrenal fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that supports gut health and adrenal function.
Seeds: Containing saponins, which help reduce inflammation and promote hormone regulation.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried arctium lappa root and pour it into a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Bring 8 ounces of filtered water to a boil and then reduce the heat to low for 5-7 minutes steeping.
Allow the tea mixture to cool down slightly before straining the leaves and discarding them.
Transfer the tea liquid to a cup and add one tablespoon of honey for sweetening if desired.
Enjoy the warm and comforting arctium lappa tea within the next 10-15 minutes after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover teas helps with adrenal fatigue because of its unique ability to support the body's natural stress response. The tea contains isoflavones, specifically biochanin A, which have been shown to regulate cortisol levels and promote a healthy balance of hormones in the adrenal glands. Additionally, red clover's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress and promote relaxation, reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being. This makes it an effective natural remedy for managing symptoms associated with adrenal fatigue.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover contains isoflavones, which can help regulate cortisol levels and alleviate symptoms of adrenal fatigue by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.

Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens present in red clover tea have been shown to help balance estrogen levels, which can be disrupted in individuals with adrenal fatigue, leading to a reduction in cortisol production and other symptoms.

Antioxidants: Red clover tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body from free radical damage caused by chronic stress, a common contributor to adrenal fatigue, and promote overall health and well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in isoflavones, which help to calm and regulate the adrenal glands, reducing stress and anxiety.
Flowers: Containing bioactive compounds like triterpenoids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and support adrenal function.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried red clover flowers and pour boiling water.
Measure out two teaspoons of fresh or dried red clover flowers and add.
Combine one teaspoon of dried red clover flowers with one cup of boiling.
Boil one cup of water and then pour it over two teaspoons of dried red.
Steep one tablespoon of fresh or dried red clover flowers in boiling water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with adrenal fatigue because of its calming and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, also known as calendula, contains flavonoids and carotenoids that help to soothe the nervous system and reduce stress levels. When consumed as a tea, these compounds work to calm an overactive hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis, which is often responsible for adrenal fatigue. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, pot marigold teas may help alleviate symptoms of adrenal fatigue, such as anxiety, insomnia, and fatigue, allowing the body to restore its natural balance and function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marigold tea helps reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms of adrenal fatigue such as joint pain and swelling, by reducing prostaglandins and leukotrienes that cause inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: Marigold tea's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that damage adrenal glands and other tissues, supporting overall adrenal health and reducing oxidative stress caused by chronic stress.

Adrenal adaptogenic properties: Marigold tea is believed to have adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress, regulating cortisol levels and promoting a balanced response to stressors, which can help alleviate symptoms of adrenal fatigue such as fatigue, anxiety, and insomnia.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots help to stabilize and calm the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
Seeds: Containing saponins and flavonoids, seeds have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and protect the adrenals from damage caused by chronic stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers in the morning for maximum potency.
Combine one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers or two teaspoons of fresh petals with boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the bioactive compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Consume one to two cups of the warm calendula tea three times daily, ideally with meals, for a minimum of six weeks.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to adrenal fatigue that are treated with herbal teas are stress-related conditions, such as anxiety and insomnia. Herbal teas like chamomile, lavender, and valerian root have been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. Other herbs like ashwagandha, rhodiola, and ginseng are believed to help alleviate fatigue and increase energy levels by supporting adrenal function and reducing cortisol production.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for adrenal fatigue are a natural remedy to help alleviate symptoms of this condition. They work by reducing stress, regulating cortisol levels, and promoting overall well-being. The best herbal teas for adrenal fatigue include Rhodiola rosea, Withania somnifera, Schisandra chinensis, Panax ginseng, Lavandula angustifolia, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Valeriana officinalis, Eleutherococcus senticosus, Passiflora incarnata, and Ocimum sanctum. If left untreated, adrenal fatigue can severely impact daily life, leading to chronic fatigue, insomnia, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting relationships, work performance, and overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with adrenal fatigue is a blend of Ashwagandha, Holy Basil, and Ginkgo Biloba. Ashwagandha, an adaptogenic herb, helps regulate cortisol levels and reduces stress. Holy Basil, also known as Tulsi, supports the body's natural response to stress and promotes a sense of calm. Ginkgo Biloba enhances circulation and improves oxygenation to the adrenal glands, promoting healing and recovery. Drinking these teas together may help alleviate symptoms of adrenal fatigue and promote overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rhodiola rosea teas helps with adrenal fatigue because it supports the body's natural response to stress. By promoting the production of neurotransmitters like serotonin and dopamine, Rhodiola rosea teas can help alleviate fatigue and improve mood. Its adaptogenic properties enable the body to adapt to changing demands, reducing the strain on the adrenal glands. This results in increased energy levels, enhanced mental clarity, and a reduced feeling of exhaustion, making Rhodiola rosea teas a popular choice for individuals struggling with adrenal fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Rosavin: Helps with adrenal fatigue by stimulating the production of neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, which are essential for regulating stress response and fatigue.
Salidroside: Aids in alleviating adrenal fatigue by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to fatigue and exhaustion.

Tyrosol: Plays a key role in reducing the negative effects of adrenal fatigue by increasing the production of catecholamines, such as adrenaline and noradrenaline, which help regulate energy and motivation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the most commonly used part of Rhodiola rosea to make teas for adrenal fatigue, as they contain the highest concentration of active compounds.
Leaves: Leaves are another commonly used part of Rhodiola rosea, as they contain some of the plant's bioactive compounds and are often used in herbal teas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rhodiola rosea roots in the early morning after the first frost but before the plant blooms.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours.
Weigh 2-4 grams of dried root per 8 ounces of water and add to a tea infuser.
Steep the dried root in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea and discard the root, then serve the rhodiola rosea tea immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera teas helps with adrenal fatigue because it supports the body's ability to manage stress and cortisol levels. The herb is believed to have adaptogenic properties, helping the adrenal glands to function more efficiently and recover from exhaustion. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Withania somnifera teas may help alleviate symptoms associated with adrenal fatigue, such as fatigue, brain fog, and sleep disturbances. This can lead to improved energy and overall well-being. Regular consumption of Withania somnifera teas may also help to rebuild and restore adrenal function.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroidal lactones help with adrenal fatigue by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby supporting the adrenal glands' ability to function properly.

Withaferin A: This alkaloid helps with adrenal fatigue by modulating the body's stress response, reducing cortisol levels, and promoting the regulation of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis.

Withanosides: These withanolide glycosides help with adrenal fatigue by enhancing the production of cortisol and other adrenal hormones, supporting the adrenal glands' function, and improving overall adrenal health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help in reducing cortisol levels and promoting adrenal function.
Leaves: Contain adaptogenic properties that help in balancing the body's response to stress and promoting overall well-being.
Fruits: Have antioxidant and adaptogenic properties that help in reducing oxidative stress and promoting adrenal health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-7 grams of dried withania somnifera roots from a reputable source.
Measure 250ml of boiling water and pour it over the dried roots.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot.
Discard the solids and serve the tea hot or allow it to cool completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Schisandra chinensis teas helps with adrenal fatigue because it supports the body's natural ability to cope with stress. This adaptogenic herb promotes balance in the adrenal glands, which become exhausted from chronic stress. Schisandra chinensis contains compounds that help regulate cortisol levels, improve energy production, and enhance the body's resilience to stress. Regular consumption of Schisandra chinensis tea may also help to reduce fatigue, improve mental clarity, and promote a sense of overall well-being in individuals experiencing adrenal fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Schisandrins: These lignans help alleviate adrenal fatigue by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the adrenal glands, promoting their recovery and function.
Schisandrone: This phenolic compound helps to improve adrenal function by enhancing the activity of adrenal enzymes and promoting the production of cortisol and other essential hormones.

Schisandrin B: This lignan has anti-stress and adaptogenic properties, helping to regulate the body's response to stress and promoting the balance of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis, which is often impaired in cases of adrenal fatigue.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used due to their high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help to support adrenal function and overall well-being.
Leaves: The leaves are utilized for their adaptogenic properties, which help to balance and support the body's response to stress.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their rich content of schisandrins, a group of lignans that have been shown to support adrenal function and reduce fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Gather 6-8 dried schisandra chinensis berries for each serving.
Combine 6-8 dried schisandra chinensis berries with 1 cup boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has cooled slightly.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Serve the tea immediately and enjoy the herbal benefits of schisandra chinensis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng teas helps with adrenal fatigue because it supports the body's natural ability to adapt to stress. Ginseng has been traditionally used to promote energy and vitality by stimulating the adrenal glands to produce cortisol, a hormone that helps regulate the body's response to stress. By increasing cortisol production, Panax ginseng teas can help alleviate symptoms of adrenal fatigue, such as fatigue, brain fog, and decreased motivation. Regular consumption of Panax ginseng teas can help the body recover from chronic stress.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins help with adrenal fatigue by reducing cortisol levels, improving adrenal function, and enhancing the body's ability to cope with stress.
Eleutherosides: These phenolic glycosides have adaptogenic properties, which help regulate the body's response to stress, support adrenal function, and promote a sense of balance and well-being.

Saponins: These triterpenoid compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate fatigue and improve overall adrenal function by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The primary part used, as they contain the highest concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to have adaptogenic properties to help alleviate adrenal fatigue.
Leaves: Used for their secondary ginsenoside content and to provide a more subtle, yet still supportive, effect on the body's stress response.
Buds: Employed for their high concentration of ginsenosides and potential antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 ounces of dried panax ginseng roots with a fine mesh strainer to sift out debris.
Combine the dried panax ginseng roots with 8 ounces of boiling water in a large heat-resistant teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes or according to the desired herbal tea strength.
Strain the liquid using a fine mesh strainer into a cup to remove the panax ginseng roots.
Allow the herbal tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before serving and consuming immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas helps with adrenal fatigue because it promotes relaxation and calms the nervous system, allowing the adrenals to recover from chronic stress. The tea's calming properties, primarily attributed to linalool and linalyl acetate, have a balancing effect on the body's hormonal response. By reducing cortisol levels and promoting a state of relaxation, Lavandula angustifolia tea creates an environment conducive to adrenal recovery. This can lead to improved energy levels, reduced fatigue, and enhanced overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with adrenal fatigue by promoting relaxation and reducing stress, allowing the adrenal glands to recover from overstimulation.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene helps with adrenal fatigue by reducing anxiety and stress levels, which can contribute to adrenal fatigue, and promoting a sense of calmness.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid helps with adrenal fatigue by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to adrenal fatigue, and supporting overall adrenal health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and volatile oils, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce stress.
Flowers: Contain linalool and linalyl acetate, two key compounds that have a soothing effect on the body and promote relaxation.
Stems: May be used to make a tea that is rich in calcium, magnesium, and potassium, which can help to regulate adrenal function and reduce stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Rinse the dried flowers with cold filtered water to remove any impurities and debris instantly.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve to remove the dried flowers and any remaining debris quickly.
Serve the tea hot or chilled depending on your preference and enjoy the calming effects instantly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas helps with adrenal fatigue because they contain licorice root, which has been found to support the adrenal glands and help alleviate symptoms of fatigue. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties may also aid in reducing stress and promoting relaxation. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to help regulate cortisol levels, balancing the body's hormonal response to stress. By providing this natural support, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas may help the body recover from adrenal fatigue, promoting overall well-being and vitality.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help with adrenal fatigue by reducing cortisol breakdown and increasing the production of cortisol in the body, thereby alleviating adrenal fatigue symptoms.
Flavonoids: They act as antioxidants and help to reduce oxidative stress, which is often associated with adrenal fatigue, thereby supporting adrenal function and overall well-being.

Glycyrrhizin: It helps to reduce stress and promote adrenal function by inhibiting the activity of the enzyme 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, which breaks down cortisol, allowing for its increased production and utilization in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps alleviate adrenal fatigue by reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation.
Leaves: They have anti-inflammatory properties and are used to soothe and calm the body, which can be beneficial for individuals with adrenal fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots weighing approximately 30 grams.
Grind the glycyrrhiza glabra roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 2 teaspoons of the powder with 1 liter of boiling water for infusion.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5 to 10 minutes in a covered container.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh sieve into a cup.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas helps with adrenal fatigue because it contains adaptogenic compounds that support the body's natural response to stress. The tea's active ingredients, including valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, promote relaxation and reduce cortisol levels, thereby alleviating symptoms of adrenal fatigue such as fatigue, insomnia, and anxiety. By calming the nervous system and regulating cortisol production, Valeriana officinalis teas help the adrenal glands to recover and function optimally, restoring balance to the body's energy and hormonal systems.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleric acid: Helps to reduce stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation and decreasing cortisol levels, which is beneficial for adrenal fatigue recovery.
Valerenic acid: Has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm the mind and body, reducing symptoms of adrenal fatigue such as fatigue and insomnia.
Valerenol: Acts as a GABA receptor agonist, which can help to regulate the body's response to stress and promote a sense of calm, thereby alleviating symptoms of adrenal fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The primary part used due to its high concentration of valerenic acid, a key compound for calming the nervous system.
Leaves: Used to provide a more subtle, less sedative effect, as they contain lower levels of valerenic acid compared to the roots.
Roots (specifically the rhizomes): Sometimes referred to as rhizomes, the underground stems are used to make teas that promote relaxation and reduce stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 valeriana officinalis roots or rhizomes in the fall after the plant has completed its growth cycle.
Wash the harvested roots in cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm dry place or using a dehydrator at 150°f for 8 hours.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried valeriana officinalis root in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool before consuming 1-2 cups per day as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eleutherococcus senticosus teas helps with adrenal fatigue because it stimulates the body's natural ability to adapt to stress. The herb contains eleutherosides, which help regulate cortisol levels and improve the body's response to stress. By promoting the production of energy-rich ATP, Eleutherococcus senticosus teas support the adrenal glands, allowing them to function more efficiently. This leads to improved energy levels, enhanced mental clarity, and a reduced feeling of exhaustion, making it an effective natural remedy for individuals experiencing adrenal fatigue. Regular consumption can also boost the immune system.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to adrenal fatigue by protecting the adrenal glands from damage.
Flavonoids: Act as a natural antioxidant, reducing cortisol levels and improving the overall function of the adrenal glands.
Lignans: Have a regulatory effect on the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, helping to restore balance to the adrenal glands and alleviate fatigue symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of eleutherosides, a group of compounds believed to have adaptogenic properties that help the body respond to stress.
Barks: The bark of Eleutherococcus senticosus is also rich in eleutherosides and has been traditionally used to support adrenal function and overall well-being.
Leaves: Leaves of the plant contain eleutherosides and other bioactive compounds that may help alleviate symptoms associated with adrenal fatigue, such as fatigue and stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of fresh or dried eleutherococcus senticosus roots or 15-30 grams of dried leaves.
Clean the harvested material with filtered water to remove dirt and debris for 5 minutes.
Dry the cleaned material at a low temperature of 35-40 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of the dried eleutherococcus senticosus material per 8 ounces of water.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas helps with adrenal fatigue because it promotes relaxation and reduces cortisol levels. The herb contains flavonoids and alkaloids that interact with the brain's GABA receptors, calming the nervous system and mitigating stress. This, in turn, allows the adrenal glands to recover from chronic stress, thereby alleviating fatigue. By promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, Passiflora incarnata teas provide a natural remedy to support adrenal function and overall well-being. Regular consumption may help to restore balance to the body's stress response system.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: Helps with adrenal fatigue by acting as a central nervous system depressant, reducing stress and anxiety caused by adrenal exhaustion.
Isophthalic acid: Contributes to alleviating adrenal fatigue symptoms by acting as an antioxidant, neutralizing free radicals that can damage adrenal glands and disrupt their function.
Isovitexin: Assists in addressing adrenal fatigue by exhibiting sedative and anxiolytic properties, helping to regulate the body's response to stress and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which are believed to have a calming effect on the nervous system.
Flowers: Used for their sedative and anti-anxiety properties, as they are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that may help reduce stress.
Roots: Used for their adaptogenic properties, as they contain compounds that may help the body adapt to stress and support adrenal function.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers in sufficient quantities for tea preparation.
Steep the flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a fine mesh to remove the flowers and sediment.
Discard the solids and transfer the liquid to a cup for immediate consumption.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in a cool dark place for future use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ocimum sanctum teas helps with adrenal fatigue because of its adaptogenic properties, which allow it to help the body adapt to stress. The tea's active compounds, such as ursolic acid and rosmarinic acid, have been shown to help regulate cortisol levels and reduce inflammation in the body. By reducing oxidative stress and promoting relaxation, Ocimum sanctum teas can help alleviate symptoms of adrenal fatigue, such as fatigue, insomnia, and decreased mental clarity. This can lead to improved energy and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with adrenal fatigue.
Beta-sitosterol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, supporting adrenal function and reducing fatigue.
Ursane triterpenoids: Possesses adaptogenic properties, helping to regulate the body's response to stress and promoting balance in the adrenal system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high concentration of antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds, which help to reduce stress and promote adrenal health.
Buds: The buds are used because they contain a higher concentration of essential oils and flavonoids, which can help to calm the nervous system and support adrenal function.
Stems: The stems are used due to their ability to provide a gentle, sustained energy boost while also promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried leaves in a tea infuser or directly in a cup.
Heat 8 ounces of water to a boil in a kettle or on the stovetop.
Steep the leaves in the hot water for 5 to 7 minutes to allow flavors to release.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot to remove solids.
Serve the tea hot or iced and enjoy immediately within 10 minutes of preparation.
